name: Chorizo Tamales with Pasilla-Corn Salsa
recipeIngredient: 10 tablespoons Vegetable shortening, at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the vegetable oil over medium-high heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté for 2 minutes until translucent.
2. Add the chorizo to the pan and continue cooking for an additional 5 minutes, or until it is cooked through.
3. Drain the oil, reserving the onions and chorizo.
4. To make the dough, beat the vegetable shortening with an electric mixer until light and fluffy. Sift in the dry ingredients (cornmeal, baking powder, cayenne, ground cumin, salt) into another bowl and mix well.
5. Gradually add the chicken stock to the dry mixture, stirring to form a soft dough.
6. Add the dough to the shortening while whisking; the mixture will be quite sticky.
7. Drain the corn husks and pat them dry with a towel.
8. Tear 12 strips (about 1/6-inch wide) from two of the husks for tying the tamales.
9. Place two husks together, overlapping one end by about 2 inches. Repeat with the remaining husks.
10. Divide the dough evenly among the six double-husks and spread it in the center, leaving a 1-inch border at each end uncovered.
11. Spread the cooked chorizo mixture on top of the dough and roll up the corn husks so that the filling is completely enclosed. Twist and tie each end with the prepared strips.
12. Steam the tamales using a conventional steamer, strainer, or vegetable basket placed in a saucepan covered with a tight-fitting lid. Ensure little to no steam escapes during cooking.
13. Steam for 30 to 35 minutes until the water is lightly boiling and the tamales are cooked through. The dough should easily release from the husk when done.
